tldr: Move from CARTO to OpenCage only if your real need is geocoding (address <-> lat/lng) rather than full spatial analytics — OpenCage is a focused geocoding API, not a map/analytics platform.
why_switch: Lower cost and simpler pricing for pure geocoding workloads.
OSM-aggregated data with clear licensing posture.
Plain REST API with minimal lock-in — easy to swap providers later.
Good fit when you don't need map rendering or SQL analytics.
what_to_export: Address lists you currently geocode through CARTO
Any datasets that only used CARTO for forward/reverse geocoding
API key inventory and where keys are referenced
Server-side code paths calling CARTO geocoding
Audit logs for compliance, if any
export_method: Pull existing geocoded results out of your CARTO PostGIS tables (SELECT to CSV/JSON). For unprocessed address lists, export them from your application database. CARTO is PostGIS-backed so standard SQL export works; consult CARTO's official docs for the current export UI.
import_method: OpenCage is a stateless REST geocoding API, so there is no bulk "import" — instead, point your code at the OpenCage forward/reverse geocoding endpoint with your API key and re-geocode addresses as needed (or backfill in batches respecting rate limits). Check OpenCage's official docs for current endpoint URLs, request limits, and response schema.

concept_mapping.note: OpenCage is stateless — you store results.
Do those in your warehouse, not OpenCage.

gotchas: OpenCage is geocoding only — if you used CARTO for maps, tiles, or SQL, you'll need separate replacements.
Respect OpenCage's rate limits when backfilling large address lists.
Result schema differs from CARTO's — adjust parsers (components, formatted, confidence).
OSM-derived data quality varies by region; spot-check critical markets.
Cache aggressively; geocoding the same address twice wastes quota.
Confirm licensing/attribution requirements in OpenCage's terms.

email.subject: Migrating geocoding from CARTO to OpenCage
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to move just the geocoding portion of our CARTO usage to OpenCage. We don't need CARTO's full spatial analytics for that workflow, and OpenCage gives us a simpler REST API and clearer pricing.

Plan:
1. Inventory every place we call CARTO for forward/reverse geocoding.
2. Export already-geocoded results from CARTO so we don't re-geocode unnecessarily.
3. Swap calls to OpenCage's REST endpoints with proper rate limiting and caching.
4. Adjust parsing for OpenCage's response shape (components, confidence, formatted).
5. Keep CARTO around for any remaining spatial-SQL or map rendering until we replace those separately.

Estimated effort for the geocoding swap alone: 4-12 hours.

Thanks
